data.name: Patcher_FillAllPaths
data.value: Please fill in all file paths.

data.name: N64Conv_Description
data.value: Convert between N64 ROM byte orders: .z64 (Big Endian), .n64 (Little Endian), .v64 (Byte-swapped).

data.name: FormatConv_Description
data.value: Convert ROM formats: add/remove copier headers, strip iNES headers, or fix NES header bytes.

data.name: Checksum_SelectFile
data.value: Select a file to calculate checksums...

data.name: DatVerifier_Description
data.value: Verify ROM files against No-Intro or TOSEC DAT files to confirm authentic, unmodified dumps.

data.name: DatVerifier_SelectDat
data.value: Select a No-Intro or TOSEC .dat file...

data.name: DumpVerifier_Description
data.value: Check ROM dump integrity by detecting overdumps, underdumps, blank regions, bad headers, and other common issues.

data.name: Duplicates_Description
data.value: Scan a directory for duplicate ROM files by CRC32 hash.

data.name: Duplicates_SelectFolder
data.value: Select a folder to scan for duplicates...

data.name: HeaderExport_Description
data.value: Export ROM header information and metadata to a text report or CSV file.

data.name: SnesHeader_Description
data.value: Add or remove the 512-byte copier header found in some SNES ROM dumps.

data.name: HeaderFixer_Description
data.value: Fix ROM headers in batch: recalculate internal checksums for SNES, Game Boy, GBA, Mega Drive/Genesis, SMS/Game Gear, and N64. Also validates headers for NES, Atari 7800, Atari Lynx, PC Engine, Virtual Boy, Neo Geo Pocket, Atari Jaguar, MSX, ColecoVision, and Watara Supervision.

data.name: Trimmer_Description
data.value: Remove trailing padding bytes (0x00 / 0xFF) from ROM files to reduce file size.

data.name: Trimmer_SelectFile
data.value: Select a ROM file to trim...

data.name: CheatCode_Description
data.value: Decode and encode Game Genie and Pro Action Replay cheat codes for retro consoles.

data.name: EmuConfig_Description
data.value: Generate configuration files for popular retro gaming emulators.

data.name: MetaScraper_Description
data.value: Scrape metadata from ROM files in bulk — extract headers, checksums, titles, regions, and system info.

data.name: MetaScraper_SelectDir
data.value: Select a directory containing ROM files...

data.name: Settings_LanguageDescription
data.value: Select the display language for the application. Changes take effect immediately.

data.name: Settings_RestartNote
data.value: Status messages in progress may still show the previous language until the next action.

data.name: Archive_Description
data.value: Extract ROMs from ZIP, RAR, 7z, and GZip archives, or create ZIP archives from ROM files.

data.name: Archive_SelectArchive
data.value: Select a ZIP, RAR, or 7z file...

data.name: Archive_SelectRomDirectory
data.value: Select a directory of ROM files...

data.name: Archive_OnePerFile
data.value: Create one archive per ROM file

data.name: SplitRom_Description
data.value: Reassemble split ROM files (.001/.002, .part1/.part2, .z01/.z02) into a single file.

data.name: Decompress_Description
data.value: Decompress GZip-compressed ROM files. Supports single file or batch decompression from a directory.

data.name: Security_Description
data.value: Analyze ROM files for security features including region locking, copy protection mechanisms, and checksum integrity.

data.name: Renamer_Description
data.value: Rename ROM files based on header-detected game titles, regions, and system information.

data.name: Renamer_SelectFile
data.value: Select a ROM file or folder to rename...

data.name: Organizer_Description
data.value: Scan a folder for ROMs, then organize them into system-specific subfolders. Choose to organize all systems or a single system, and select whether to copy or move files.

data.name: Organizer_SelectOutput
data.value: Select the destination folder for organized ROMs...

data.name: Organize_DialogDescription
data.value: Choose whether to copy or move the ROMs into system-specific folders.

data.name: PatchCreator_Description
data.value: Create IPS patches by comparing an original ROM with a modified version.

data.name: HexViewer_Description
data.value: View ROM file contents in hexadecimal with ASCII sidebar. Navigate and search through file bytes.

data.name: HexViewer_SelectFile
data.value: Select a file to view in hex...

data.name: SaveConv_Description
data.value: Convert save files between formats: swap endianness, pad to power-of-two, trim trailing bytes, or convert between .sav and .srm.

data.name: BatchHash_Description
data.value: Calculate checksums for all ROM files in a directory. Export results as CSV, SFV, MD5, or text reports.

data.name: BatchHash_SelectFolder
data.value: Select a folder containing ROM files...

data.name: DatFilter_Description
data.value: Filter No-Intro and Redump DAT files by region, language, and category. Apply 1G1R (One Game, One ROM) deduplication to keep only the best version of each game.

data.name: DatFilter_SelectDat
data.value: Select a No-Intro or Redump .dat file...

data.name: DatFilter_RegionHint
data.value: Comma-separated, highest priority first (e.g. USA, Europe, Japan, World)

data.name: DatFilter_LanguageHint
data.value: Comma-separated, highest priority first (e.g. En, Fr, De, Es, Ja)

data.name: MameAuditor_Description
data.value: Audit MAME ROM sets against a MAME XML database. Verifies ZIP-packaged ROM sets for completeness, correct checksums, and proper file sizes.

data.name: MameAuditor_SelectXml
data.value: Select a MAME XML or DAT file (from mame -listxml)...

data.name: MameAuditor_SelectRomDir
data.value: Select the directory containing MAME ROM ZIPs...

data.name: MameChd_Description
data.value: Verify MAME CHD (Compressed Hunks of Data) file integrity. Reads and validates CHD v3, v4, and v5 headers including SHA-1 checksums, compression type, and logical size.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchDescription
data.value: Configure RetroArch to launch ROMs directly from the ROM Browser. You can browse for an existing installation or download RetroArch.

data.name: MameChdConv_Description
data.value: Compress disc images (CUE/BIN/ISO/GDI) to CHD format or decompress CHD files back to raw disc images. Requires chdman (part of MAME tools) to be installed and available in your system PATH.

data.name: MameChdConv_SelectDiscDir
data.value: Select a directory containing disc images...

data.name: MameChdConv_SelectChdDir
data.value: Select a directory containing CHD files...

data.name: MameDatEditor_Description
data.value: Load, edit, and save MAME DAT files in Logiqx XML format. Modify header metadata, add or remove game entries, and edit game properties such as name, description, year, and manufacturer.

data.name: MameDatEditor_SelectDat
data.value: Select a DAT or XML file to edit...

data.name: MameDatEditor_SearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search by game name or description...

data.name: MameRebuilder_Description
data.value: Rebuild MAME ROM sets from scattered or loose ROM files. Indexes a source directory by CRC32 and creates properly structured ZIP archives matching the MAME database.

data.name: MameRebuilder_SelectXml
data.value: Select a MAME XML or DAT file (from mame -listxml)...

data.name: MameRebuilder_SelectSourceDir
data.value: Select the directory containing source ROM files...

data.name: MameRebuilder_SelectOutputDir
data.value: Select the output directory for rebuilt ROM sets...

data.name: MameRebuilder_ModeSplit
data.value: Split (each machine has only its own ROMs)

data.name: MameRebuilder_ModeNonMerged
data.value: Non-Merged (each machine has all ROMs it needs)

data.name: MameDir2Dat_Description
data.value: Create a DAT file from a directory of ROM files. Scans ZIP archives and loose files, computes checksums, and exports in Logiqx XML format compatible with CLRMamePro and other ROM managers.

data.name: MameDir2Dat_SelectRomDir
data.value: Select the directory containing ROM files...

data.name: MameSamples_Description
data.value: Audit MAME sample audio files against a MAME XML database. Verifies that sample ZIP archives contain the expected WAV files for each machine.

data.name: MameSamples_SelectXml
data.value: Select a MAME XML or DAT file (from mame -listxml)...

data.name: MameSamples_SelectSampleDir
data.value: Select the directory containing MAME sample ZIPs...

data.name: Settings_CoreDownloaderDescription
data.value: Download and update RetroArch libretro cores. Click Check Cores to see installed status, then download any missing cores.

data.name: Browser_SearchWatermark
data.value: Search by name, system, or size...

data.name: HostShare_Subtitle
data.value: Start a local HTTP server to share ROMs with others on your network.

data.name: HostShare_NoFilesToHost
data.value: No files or directory to host.

data.name: HostShare_ServerRunning
data.value: Server is running. Share the URL with others on your network.

data.name: Settings_UpdateDescription
data.value: Check for new versions of Retro Multi Tools from GitHub releases.

data.name: Settings_UpdateAutoCheck
data.value: Automatically check for updates on startup

data.name: Settings_UpdateUpToDate
data.value: ✔ You are running the latest version.

data.name: Settings_UpdateAvailableMessage
data.value: A new version is available: {0} → {1}. Would you like to visit the download page?

data.name: Settings_UpdateNetworkError
data.value: ✘ Network error while checking for updates

data.name: Settings_UpdateInstalling
data.value: Installing update… The application will restart.

data.name: Settings_UpdateDownloadError
data.value: ✘ Failed to download the update. Please try again later.

data.name: Settings_UpdateInstallError
data.value: ✘ Failed to install the update. The updater could not be started.

data.name: Settings_MinimizeToTrayOnLaunch
data.value: Minimize to tray when launching a ROM

data.name: BigPicture_Ready
data.value: Select a folder to browse your ROM library.

data.name: BigPicture_Hints
data.value: Arrow Keys: Navigate | Enter: Launch | F: Favorite | R: Random | I: ROM Info | +/−: Zoom | H: Help | Esc: Exit

data.name: BigPicture_HintsPad
data.value: D-Pad: Navigate | A: Launch | Y: Favorite | Back: Random | B: ROM Info | Start: Help | Guide: Exit

data.name: BigPicture_EmptyState
data.value: Select a ROM folder to start browsing your library in Big Picture Mode.

data.name: Settings_BigPictureDescription
data.value: A fullscreen, controller-friendly mode for browsing and launching ROMs.

data.name: Settings_BigPictureAutoStart
data.value: Start in Big Picture Mode automatically

data.name: Settings_BigPictureFolderPlaceholder
data.value: ROM folder for Big Picture Mode

data.name: Browser_SelectRomForArtwork
data.value: Select a ROM to view artwork

data.name: BigPicture_HelpDismiss
data.value: Press H, ?, or Escape to close

data.name: BigPicture_HelpRomInfo
data.value: Show ROM info for selected ROM

data.name: BigPicture_RomInfoDismiss
data.value: Press I or Escape to close

data.name: BigPicture_RomInfoNoGoodTools
data.value: No GoodTools codes identified in the filename.

data.name: BigPicture_SystemNotSupported
data.value: System '{0}' is not supported for RetroArch launch.

data.name: Settings_ControllerProfilesDescription
data.value: Download or update the community SDL game controller database (gamecontrollerdb.txt). This enables automatic recognition of hundreds of game controllers in Big Picture Mode and RetroArch.

data.name: Settings_ControllerProfilesNotInstalled
data.value: No controller profiles installed. Click the button below to download them.

data.name: Settings_GamepadToolDescription
data.value: Create or modify gamepad mappings for controllers that are not automatically recognised. Mappings are saved locally and applied automatically.

data.name: Settings_GamepadToolNoControllers
data.value: No controllers detected. Connect a controller and click Refresh.

data.name: Settings_GamepadToolMappingHelp
data.value: Click "Start Mapping" and follow the prompts to assign each button and axis. Press Skip to leave an element unmapped.

data.name: GoodTools_Description
data.value: Identify GoodTools labelling conventions (Country Codes, Standard Codes, and GoodGen-Specific Codes) from ROM filenames. Supports single ROM or batch directory processing.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchFileNotFound
data.value: ✘ File not found at configured path.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchInvalidBundle
data.value: ✘ Selected .app bundle does not contain a valid RetroArch executable.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchNotFound
data.value: ✘ RetroArch not found. Please browse manually or download it.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchDownloadPageOpened
data.value: RetroArch download page opened. After installing, use Auto-Detect or Browse to configure the path.

data.name: Settings_RetroArchCouldNotOpenBrowser
data.value: Could not open browser. Please visit: {0}

data.name: Settings_RetroArchNotConfigured
data.value: ✘ RetroArch not found. Configure the path first.

data.name: Settings_SelectRomFolderBigPicture
data.value: Select ROM Folder for Big Picture Mode

data.name: Cheat_EnterCode
data.value: Please enter a cheat code to decode.

data.name: Cheat_EnterAddressValue
data.value: Please enter both address and value in hexadecimal.

data.name: Cheat_InvalidHex
data.value: Invalid hex values. Please enter valid hexadecimal numbers.

data.name: Cheat_ValueOutOfRange
data.value: Value out of range. Address should be 16/24-bit, Value should be 8-bit (00-FF).

data.name: Hex_SearchingFromStart
data.value: Wrapping search to beginning of file...

data.name: Inspector_DoneNoArtwork
data.value: Done — no artwork found for this title.

data.name: Trimmer_AlreadyTrimmed
data.value: No padding found — file is already trimmed.

data.name: Trimmer_SelectInputFile
data.value: Please select an input ROM file.

data.name: Trimmer_SelectOutputFile
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: N64_UnknownFormat
data.value: Unknown — not a recognized N64 ROM

data.name: N64_SelectInputFile
data.value: Please select an input ROM file.

data.name: N64_SelectOutputFile
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: Gamepad_SdlInitFailed
data.value: SDL2 could not be initialised. Gamepad mapping is unavailable.

data.name: Gamepad_SdlNotFound
data.value: SDL2 library not found. Please install SDL2 on your system.

data.name: Gamepad_FailedToOpen
data.value: Failed to open joystick for mapping.

data.name: Duplicate_ConfirmDelete
data.value: ⚠️ This will permanently delete {0} duplicate file(s) and free ~{1}. The first file in each group will be kept.

data.name: Duplicate_FoundGroups
data.value: Found {0} duplicate group(s). {1} wasted by duplicates.

data.name: Split_NoParts
data.value: No split pattern detected for this file.

data.name: Split_SelectInputFile
data.value: Please select the first split part file.

data.name: Split_SelectOutputFile
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: Archive_NoRomsFound
data.value: No ROM files found in this archive.

data.name: Archive_SelectInputOutput
data.value: Please select input and output paths.

data.name: GoodTools_SelectFile
data.value: Please select a ROM file or directory.

data.name: SnesHeader_SelectInputFile
data.value: Please select an input ROM file.

data.name: SnesHeader_SelectOutputFile
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: Common_NeoGeoBiosNotice
data.value: Note: Using the "AES" BIOS will start games in their console (home) versions. Using the "NEOGEO" (Universe BIOS or standard MVS) BIOS will boot games in their arcade versions.

data.name: Settings_MameDescription
data.value: Configure standalone MAME to launch Arcade ROMs directly from the ROM Browser. You can browse for an existing installation or download MAME.

data.name: Settings_MameFileNotFound
data.value: ✘ Configured MAME path does not exist.

data.name: Settings_MameInvalidBundle
data.value: ✘ Selected .app bundle does not contain a valid MAME executable.

data.name: Settings_MameNotFound
data.value: MAME was not found on this system. Please install MAME or browse to it manually.

data.name: Settings_MameDownloadPageOpened
data.value: MAME download page opened in browser.

data.name: Settings_MameCouldNotOpenBrowser
data.value: Could not open browser. Visit {0} to download MAME.

data.name: Browser_MameSystemNotSupported
data.value: System '{0}' is not supported for MAME launch. MAME supports Arcade ROMs only.

data.name: Browser_MednafenSystemNotSupported
data.value: System '{0}' is not supported for Mednafen launch.

data.name: MednafenIntegration_Description
data.value: Configure Mednafen to launch ROMs directly from the ROM Browser. Mednafen supports PC Engine, Lynx, Neo Geo Pocket, Game Boy, NES, SNES, Saturn, and more.

data.name: Settings_MednafenDescription
data.value: Configure standalone Mednafen to launch ROMs directly from the ROM Browser. You can browse for an existing installation or download Mednafen.

data.name: Settings_MednafenFileNotFound
data.value: ✘ Configured Mednafen path does not exist.

data.name: Settings_MednafenInvalidBundle
data.value: ✘ Selected .app bundle does not contain a valid Mednafen executable.

data.name: Settings_MednafenNotFound
data.value: Mednafen was not found on this system. Please install Mednafen or browse to it manually.

data.name: Settings_MednafenDownloadPageOpened
data.value: Mednafen download page opened in browser.

data.name: Settings_MednafenCouldNotOpenBrowser
data.value: Could not open browser. Visit {0} to download Mednafen.

data.name: RAIntegration_Description
data.value: Configure RetroArch, manage cores, and download controller profiles.

data.name: RAAchievements_Description
data.value: Create and edit achievement definition files compatible with RetroAchievements.org. Define memory conditions, point values, and achievement metadata for your favorite games.

data.name: RAAchievements_InvalidPoints
data.value: Points must be a number between 0 and 100.

data.name: RAPlaylist_Description
data.value: Create RetroArch playlists (.lpl) from ROM directories and optionally download artwork thumbnails from the libretro-thumbnails server.

data.name: RAPlaylist_ThumbnailsDescription
data.value: Download box art, snaps, and title screen thumbnails for playlist entries from the libretro-thumbnails server.

data.name: RAPlaylist_InvalidRomDir
data.value: Please select a valid ROM directory.

data.name: RAPlaylist_NothingToSave
data.value: No playlist to save. Build or load a playlist first.

data.name: RAPlaylist_NothingToDownload
data.value: No playlist loaded. Build or load a playlist first.

data.name: RAPlaylist_RetroArchNotConfigured
data.value: RetroArch is not configured. Please set the RetroArch path in Settings.

data.name: RAPlaylist_SelectCategories
data.value: Please select at least one thumbnail category.

data.name: RAShortcut_Description
data.value: Create desktop shortcuts that launch a ROM directly in RetroArch with a specified core. Supports Windows (.lnk), Linux (.desktop), and macOS (.command) formats.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_Description
data.value: Manage your Analogue Pocket: update openFPGA cores & firmware, browse and install cores, export screenshots, backup saves, manage save states, export GB Camera photos, and more.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_SelectSdCard
data.value: Select the Analogue Pocket SD card root folder...

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_Description
data.value: Tools for the Analogue NT and Super NT: generate custom fonts and repair NES ROM headers.

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_SelectSdCard
data.value: Select the Analogue NT / Super NT SD card root folder...

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_Description
data.value: Tools for the Analogue Mega SG: generate custom fonts and convert save game files.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_SelectSdCard
data.value: Select the Analogue Mega SG SD card root folder...

data.name: Analogue3D_Description
data.value: Manage your Analogue 3D: configure label artwork, per-game display and hardware settings, and manage Game Paks on the SD card.

data.name: Analogue3D_SelectSdCard
data.value: Select the Analogue 3D SD card root folder...

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_FontDescription
data.value: Generate a custom 8×8 font for the console menu. Use the built-in default or provide a 128×128 BMP image containing a 16×16 grid of characters.

data.name: Analogue_FontSourceWatermark
data.value: Leave empty to generate default font...

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_HeaderDescription
data.value: Repair iNES ROM headers by cleaning invalid padding bytes (bytes 11–15). NES 2.0 headers are preserved and not modified.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_FontDescription
data.value: Generate a custom 8×8 font for the Mega SG menu. Use the built-in default or provide a 128×128 BMP image containing a 16×16 grid of characters.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_SavesDescription
data.value: Convert save game files between different emulator and hardware formats (endian swap, padding, trimming).

data.name: MameIntegration_Description
data.value: Configure MAME to launch Arcade ROMs directly from the ROM Browser.

data.name: Settings_GamepadDescription
data.value: Configure game controller input for Big Picture Mode. Requires SDL2.

data.name: BigPicture_ScreensaverDismiss
data.value: Press any key or button to dismiss

data.name: BigPicture_NoRecentlyPlayed
data.value: No recently played games in this collection

data.name: BigPicture_StatsDismiss
data.value: Press C or Escape to close

data.name: BigPicture_GalleryActive
data.value: Gallery mode — press G or navigate to stop

data.name: BigPicture_HelpRating
data.value: Rate game (press same key to clear)

data.name: BigPicture_ArtworkViewerDismiss
data.value: V or Esc to close · ← → cycle artwork

data.name: Settings_BigPicturePlayTracking
data.value: Enable play count and session time tracking

data.name: Browser_SystemNotSupported
data.value: System '{0}' is not supported for RetroArch launch.

data.name: Browser_SelectOrganizeOutputTitle
data.value: Select Output Folder for Organized ROMs

data.name: Browser_UseFormatConverter
data.value: To convert {0}, use the ROM Format Converter tool.

data.name: Browser_UseTrimmer
data.value: To trim {0}, use the ROM Trimmer tool.

data.name: Browser_UseVerifier
data.value: To verify {0}, use the DAT Verifier or Dump Verifier tool.

data.name: GoodTools_IdentifyComplete
data.value: ✔ Identification complete! {0} ROM(s) with GoodTools codes, {1} without.

data.name: Analogue3D_FoundGamePaks
data.value: ✔ Found {0} Game Pak(s). Click a game to edit per-game settings.

data.name: Analogue3D_InvalidSdCard
data.value: ⚠ The selected folder does not appear to be a valid Analogue 3D SD card. Please select the root of your Analogue 3D SD card.

data.name: Analogue3D_NoGamePaksFound
data.value: No N64 Game Paks found on the SD card. Add .z64/.n64/.v64 files to the N64/ folder.

data.name: Analogue3D_NoGameSelected
data.value: ✘ No game selected. Click a Game Pak to select it.

data.name: Analogue3D_SelectGamePakFirst
data.value: ⚠ Select a Game Pak first by clicking 'Scan Game Paks' then clicking a game.

data.name: Analogue3D_NoArtworkToRemove
data.value: No artwork to remove for the selected Game Pak.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_InvalidSdCard
data.value: ⚠ The selected folder does not appear to be a valid Analogue Mega SG SD card. Expected a "System" directory at the root.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_SelectSaveFirst
data.value: ✘ Please select a save file first.

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_InvalidNesRom
data.value: ✘ Please select a valid NES ROM file.

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_InvalidSdCard
data.value: ⚠ The selected folder does not appear to be a valid Analogue NT / Super NT SD card. Expected a "System" directory at the root.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_FoundFolders
data.value: ✔ Found {0} platform folder(s). Click to open.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_FoundSaveStates
data.value: ✔ Found {0} save state(s). Select states to delete.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_InvalidSdCard
data.value: ⚠ The selected folder does not appear to be a valid Analogue Pocket SD card. Please select the root of your Pocket SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoCoresFound
data.value: No installed cores found on the SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoGameFolders
data.value: No game folders found under Assets/.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoSaveStates
data.value: No save states found on the SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_SelectGbCameraTitle
data.value: Select Game Boy Camera Save File

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoScreenshots
data.value: No screenshots found on the SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoSavesFound
data.value: No save files found on the SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_RestoredSaves
data.value: ✔ Restored {0} save file(s) to the Pocket.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoSavesInBackup
data.value: No save files found in the backup folder.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoGbCameraPhotos
data.value: No photos found in the save file (slots may be empty).

data.name: AnaloguePocket_CopiedFiles
data.value: ✔ Copied {0} file(s) to the Pocket SD card.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoCopyFilesFound
data.value: No files found in the source folder.

data.name: AnaloguePocket_NoLibraryImagesNeeded
data.value: All ROMs already have library images, or no ROMs found.

data.name: DatFilter_LoadDatFirst
data.value: Please load a DAT file first.

data.name: DatFilter_LoadedEntries
data.value: Loaded {0} ROM entries from DAT file.

data.name: DatVerifier_LoadDatFirst
data.value: Please load a DAT file first.

data.name: DatVerifier_LoadedEntries
data.value: Loaded {0} ROM entries from DAT file.

data.name: DatVerifier_SelectInput
data.value: Please select a ROM file or directory.

data.name: Decompress_SelectInputOutput
data.value: Please select input and output paths.

data.name: DumpVerifier_SelectInput
data.value: Please select a ROM file or directory.

data.name: DumpVerifier_VerificationComplete
data.value: ✔ Verification complete! {0} good dumps, {1} with potential issues.

data.name: EmuConfig_RetroArchNotDetected
data.value: ✘ Could not detect RetroArch config directory. Automatic detection failed. Please configure the RetroArch path in Settings first, or ensure RetroArch is installed in a standard location.

data.name: FormatConv_SelectInputOutput
data.value: Please select input and output paths.

data.name: HeaderExport_SelectInput
data.value: Please select an input ROM file or directory.

data.name: HeaderExport_SelectOutputPath
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: HeaderFixer_SelectInputOutput
data.value: Please select input and output paths.

data.name: MameAuditor_LoadXmlFirst
data.value: Please load a MAME XML database first.

data.name: MameChdConv_SelectInputOutput
data.value: Please select input and output paths.

data.name: MameChd_SelectInput
data.value: Please select a CHD file or directory.

data.name: MameDatEditor_GameAdded
data.value: ✔ New game '{0}' added. Select it to edit.

data.name: MameDir2Dat_NoScanResults
data.value: No scan results to export. Run a scan first.

data.name: MameRebuilder_LoadXmlFirst
data.value: Please load a MAME XML database first.

data.name: MameRebuilder_SelectDirectories
data.value: Please select both source and output directories.

data.name: MameSamples_LoadXmlFirst
data.value: Please load a MAME XML database first.

data.name: MetaScraper_NoRomsFound
data.value: No ROM files found in the selected directory.

data.name: MetaScraper_SelectOutputPath
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: PatchCreator_FillAllPaths
data.value: Please fill in all file paths.

data.name: PatchCreator_PatchCreated
SaveConv_SelectInputFile
data.value: Please select an input save file.

data.name: SaveConv_SelectOutputPath
data.value: Please specify an output file path.

data.name: Security_AnalysisComplete
data.value: ✔ Analysis complete! {0} ROM(s) analyzed, {1} security feature(s) detected.

data.name: Security_SelectInput
data.value: Please select a ROM file or directory.

data.name: Hex_OffsetBeyondEnd
data.value: Offset 0x{0} is beyond end of file (0x{1}).

data.name: DatVerifier_RomVerified
DatVerifier_RomNotFound
DatFilter_FilterComplete
DumpVerifier_GoodDump
data.value: ✔ {0} appears to be a good dump!

data.name: Renamer_PreviewSummary
data.value: Preview: {0} file(s) would be renamed out of {1} scanned.

data.name: Renamer_AllMatch
data.value: No files need renaming — all names already match headers.

data.name: MameDatEditor_MoreResults
data.value: ... and {0} more (use search to narrow results)

data.name: Gamepad_HasMapping
data.value: ✔ This controller has an existing mapping.

data.name: Gamepad_NoMapping
data.value: ✘ No mapping found — use the wizard below to create one.

data.name: MameChd_SelectChdDir
data.value: Select a directory containing CHD files...

data.name: DatVerify_BatchSummary
data.value: {0} of {1} ROMs verified against {2} DAT entries. {3} not found.

data.name: DatFilter_ResultSummary
data.value: {0} of {1} entries retained. Excluded: {2} by category, {3} by 1G1R.

data.name: Detect_ErrorArchiveNoRoms
data.value: Archive does not contain files with recognized ROM extensions.

data.name: Detect_ErrorGBNoLogo
data.value: Nintendo logo bytes not found at 0x104.

data.name: Detect_ErrorMegaDriveNoSega
data.value: File has a Mega Drive extension but no valid SEGA header signature.

data.name: Detect_ErrorN64TooSmall
data.value: File too small to parse N64 header.

data.name: Detect_ErrorNoRomHeader
data.value: No recognized ROM header signature found in .bin file.

data.name: Detect_ErrorPathEmpty
data.value: File path cannot be null or empty.

data.name: Detect_ErrorZipNoRoms
data.value: ZIP archive does not contain files with recognized ROM extensions.

data.name: DumpVerify_Atari7800Sig
data.value: Atari 7800 ROM is missing ATARI7800 signature — may not have a valid header.

data.name: DumpVerify_ColecoVision
data.value: ColecoVision magic bytes are non-standard — ROM may not be a valid ColecoVision cartridge.

data.name: DumpVerify_GBALogoInvalid
data.value: GBA header logo is invalid — ROM may not boot on hardware.

data.name: DumpVerify_GBChecksum
data.value: Game Boy header checksum is invalid — ROM may be corrupt or modified.

data.name: DumpVerify_LynxHeader
data.value: Atari Lynx ROM has no LYNX header — some emulators require the header for correct operation.

data.name: DumpVerify_MegaDriveNoSega
data.value: Mega Drive ROM is missing SEGA identifier — may not be a valid ROM.

data.name: DumpVerify_MostlyUniform
data.value: File is {0} identical bytes (0x{1}). Likely a bad dump.

data.name: DumpVerify_MsxSignature
data.value: MSX cartridge lacks standard "AB" signature — may not be a valid cartridge ROM.

data.name: DumpVerify_N64NoTitle
data.value: N64 ROM is missing title in header — may not be a valid ROM.

data.name: DumpVerify_NdsCrc
data.value: Nintendo DS header CRC16 is invalid — ROM header may be corrupt.

data.name: DumpVerify_NesPrgZero
data.value: NES header declares 0 KB PRG ROM — likely invalid.

data.name: DumpVerify_NonStandardSize
data.value: ROM data size ({0}) is not a standard size. May indicate corruption.

data.name: DumpVerify_Overdump
data.value: ROM data size ({0}) is larger than expected ({1}). Possible overdump.

data.name: DumpVerify_PceHeader
data.value: PC Engine ROM has a copier header — this may cause compatibility issues with some emulators.

data.name: DumpVerify_Sega32x
data.value: Sega 32X ROM is missing SEGA identifier — may not be a valid ROM.

data.name: DumpVerify_SmsGgChecksum
data.value: SMS/Game Gear TMR SEGA header checksum is invalid — ROM may be corrupt.

data.name: DumpVerify_SnesChecksum
data.value: SNES internal checksum does not match. Header may be corrupt.

data.name: DumpVerify_TrailingFF
data.value: Last {0} of file is mostly 0xFF bytes ({1}). Possible overdump.

data.name: DumpVerify_TrailingZero
data.value: Last {0} of file is mostly 0x00 bytes ({1}). Possible overdump or padding.

data.name: DumpVerify_Underdump
data.value: ROM data size ({0}) is smaller than expected ({1}). Possible underdump.

data.name: DumpVerify_UniformBytes
data.value: Entire file contains only 0x{0} bytes. This is a blank/bad dump.

data.name: DumpVerify_VirtualBoy
data.value: Virtual Boy ROM is missing title in header — may not be a valid ROM.

data.name: MameAudit_DiskReadError
data.value: Disk {0}: could not read CHD file

data.name: MameChd_ErrorInvalidMagic
data.value: Invalid CHD magic number — not a CHD file

data.name: MameChd_ErrorTooSmall
data.value: File too small to be a valid CHD file

data.name: MameChd_ErrorUnexpectedEof
data.value: Unexpected end of file while reading CHD header

data.name: Organizer_NoRomsFound
data.value: No ROMs found in the selected folder.

data.name: RAAchievement_InvalidConsoleId
data.value: Console ID must be a positive integer.

data.name: RAAchievement_InvalidType
data.value: Achievement '{0}': Invalid type. Must be standard, missable, progression, or win_condition.

data.name: RAAchievement_InvalidGameId
data.value: Game ID must be a positive integer.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_10nesLockoutDesc
data.value: NES cartridges use the 10NES lockout chip for hardware-level copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_3doDiscEncryptDesc
data.value: 3DO uses encrypted disc headers; the console verifies disc authenticity at boot time.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_3doRegionDesc
data.value: Panasonic 3DO is region-free by design; the 3DO specification does not include region lockout mechanisms.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_3dsEncryptDesc
data.value: Nintendo 3DS uses AES encryption for game content, RSA signatures for code verification, and a unique per-console key system for digital rights management.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_3dsRegionDesc
data.value: Nintendo 3DS enforces hardware region locking; game cartridges and digital titles are locked to their region (Japan, North America, Europe, etc.).

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AmigaCd32AkikoDesc
data.value: Amiga CD32 uses the AKIKO chip for CD drive access and copy protection verification.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AmigaCd32RegionDesc
data.value: Amiga CD32 uses PAL/NTSC region encoding; some titles may not work on consoles from a different video region.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AmstradCpcNoCopy
data.value: Amstrad CPC No ROM Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AmstradCpcNoCopyDesc
data.value: No cartridge-level copy protection in ROM format; original media used various disk protection schemes.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AmstradCpcRegionDesc
data.value: Amstrad CPC has no hardware region lock; regional differences are limited to keyboard layout and language.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ArcadeBoardProtDesc
data.value: Arcade boards may use various copy protection mechanisms including encrypted ROMs, custom coprocessors, battery-backed RAM, or hardware security modules depending on the manufacturer and era.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ArcadeRegionDesc
data.value: Arcade boards typically have no hardware region lock; regional differences are implemented in software via DIP switch settings or separate ROM sets.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari2600NoCopyDesc
data.value: No software-level copy protection. The simple ROM cartridge format has no validation mechanism.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari2600RegionDesc
data.value: Atari 2600 is region-free; games are NTSC or PAL specific based on programming, not hardware lockout.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari5200NoCopyDesc
data.value: No software-level copy protection mechanism in cartridges.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari5200RegionDesc
data.value: Atari 5200 is NTSC-only; no hardware region lock since the system was only released in North America.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari7800DigSigDesc
data.value: Atari 7800 cartridges contain a digital signature that the BIOS verifies before running the game. Without a valid signature, the console falls back to Atari 2600 mode.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari7800RegionDesc
data.value: Atari 7800 uses a digital signature in the cartridge header to enforce region lockout. PAL and NTSC consoles validate this signature before running the game.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari800NoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection; original media used basic disk protection schemes.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Atari800RegionDesc
data.value: Atari 8-bit computers are region-free; NTSC and PAL differences are hardware-based, not enforced by software lockout.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AtariJaguarRegionDesc
data.value: Atari Jaguar is region-free; the Jaguar has no hardware or software region lockout mechanism.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_AtariLynxRegionDesc
data.value: Atari Lynx is region-free; the Lynx was designed as a worldwide product with no region restrictions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_CdiProtDesc
data.value: Philips CD-i uses the CD-RTOS operating system with minimal disc-level copy protection. The proprietary OS format provides basic protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_CdiRegionDesc
data.value: Philips CD-i is region-free; the CD-i standard does not include region lockout mechanisms. PAL/NTSC differences are handled by the player hardware.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_CicLockoutDesc
data.value: SNES cartridges use a CIC (Checking Integrated Circuit) lockout chip for region and copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ColecoVisionBiosDesc
data.value: ColecoVision BIOS checks for specific magic bytes (0xAA 0x55 or 0x55 0xAA) at the start of the cartridge ROM to validate the cartridge.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ColecoVisionRegionDesc
data.value: ColecoVision hardware is region-free. Cartridges from any region will work on any console.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ColorCompNoCopy
data.value: Color Computer No ROM Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ColorCompNoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection in cartridge format.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_DreamcastGdromDesc
data.value: Sega Dreamcast uses the proprietary GD-ROM disc format which stores data in a high-density area inaccessible to standard CD drives, providing physical copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_DreamcastRegionDesc
data.value: Sega Dreamcast uses area symbol codes in the disc header for region locking. Games are coded for Japan, USA, and/or Europe regions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ExecRomHandshakeDesc
data.value: Intellivision uses the EXEC ROM firmware to perform a handshake with the cartridge. The EXEC validates the cartridge entry point before transferring control.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_FairchildNoCopy
data.value: Fairchild Channel F No Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_FairchildNoCopyDesc
data.value: No copy protection. The Channel F uses simple ROM cartridges with no validation mechanism.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_GameCubeAuthDesc
data.value: GameCube uses a proprietary mini-DVD format with Burst Cutting Area (BCA) data for disc authentication. The drive firmware validates disc authenticity before allowing reads.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_GameCubeRegionLockDesc
data.value: GameCube enforces hardware-level region lockout. The console verifies the disc region code at boot time.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_HuCardFormFactorDesc
data.value: PC Engine uses the proprietary HuCard format as a physical copy protection mechanism.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_IntellivisionRegionDesc
data.value: Intellivision hardware is region-free; the same cartridges are compatible with all console variants worldwide.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_JaguarEncBootDesc
data.value: Cartridge header contains an encrypted boot sequence validated by the Jaguar BIOS.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_LynxCartEncryptDesc
data.value: Atari Lynx uses an encrypted boot header that the system ROM decrypts before execution.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_MemotechNoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection. Software was distributed on cassette and cartridge without copy protection mechanisms.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_MsxRegionDesc
data.value: MSX standard is region-free by design; cartridges are compatible across regions. Software may have language differences.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_N64ddCopyProtDesc
data.value: N64DD disks use the N64 CIC chip validation and proprietary magnetic disk format for copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_N64ddRegionDesc
data.value: Nintendo 64DD uses region locking inherited from the N64 console via the CIC chip. The 64DD peripheral was only officially released in Japan.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NdsEncryptDesc
data.value: Nintendo DS cartridges use encrypted secure area (Blowfish encryption) in the first 2 KB of ARM9 code, validated by the BIOS during boot.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NdsRegionDesc
data.value: Nintendo DS is region-free for DS games; the hardware does not enforce region lockout on DS cartridges. DSi-enhanced titles may have region restrictions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NecPc88NoCopy
data.value: NEC PC-88 No ROM Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NecPc88NoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection; original floppy disks used various disk protection schemes.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NecPc88RegionDesc
data.value: NEC PC-88 was a Japan-only platform; no hardware region lock since the system was only released domestically.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NeoGeoCdProtDesc
data.value: Neo Geo CD uses the standard CD format with minimal copy protection; some titles check for specific disc characteristics.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NeoGeoCdRegionDesc
data.value: Neo Geo CD enforces region locking via the console BIOS; discs are coded for Japan, USA, or Europe regions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NeoGeoPocketRegionDesc
data.value: Neo Geo Pocket and Pocket Color hardware is region-free. Games from any region can be played on any console.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NeoGeoProtDesc
data.value: Neo Geo AES/MVS cartridges use proprietary cartridge connectors and some later titles employ encrypted graphics data (C-ROM encryption) for copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NeoGeoRegionDesc
data.value: SNK Neo Geo AES consoles use a BIOS-level region lock (Japan, USA, Europe). MVS arcade boards use regional BIOS variants.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Nes2TimingDesc
data.value: NES 2.0 header supports region timing metadata (NTSC/PAL/Multi).

data.name: SecAnalyzer_OricNoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection; original cassette tapes used custom loading routines.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_OricRegionDesc
data.value: Oric has no region locking; the Oric was sold primarily in Europe with no regional restrictions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_PceRegionDesc
data.value: PC Engine (Japan) and TurboGrafx-16 (USA) use different hardware pin configurations for region lockout. HuCard form factor differs between regions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SMSGameGearRegionDesc
data.value: No TMR SEGA header found — region cannot be determined from the ROM header. Early cartridges lack this marker.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SaturnDiscProtDesc
data.value: Sega Saturn uses a security ring on the outer edge of the disc for copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SaturnRegionDesc
data.value: Sega Saturn enforces region lockout via a region code in the disc header. Games are typically locked to Japan, North America, or Europe.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SegaCdDiscProtDesc
data.value: Region-coded disc format with security ring on physical media.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SmsBiosCheckDesc
data.value: Master System and Game Gear consoles use a BIOS check that verifies the "TMR SEGA" header marker to validate cartridges.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ThomsonNoCopy
data.value: Thomson MO5 No ROM Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ThomsonNoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection; original media used format-based copy protection.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_ThomsonRegionDesc
data.value: Thomson MO5 has no region locking; the Thomson MO5 was primarily a French market computer.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_TigerNoCopy
data.value: Tiger Game Com No Copy Protection

data.name: SecAnalyzer_TigerNoCopyDesc
data.value: No ROM-level copy protection. The Game Com uses simple ROM cartridges.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Trs80RegionDesc
data.value: TRS-80 Color Computer has no region locking; the system was primarily sold in North America.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_VirtualBoyLogoDesc
data.value: Virtual Boy validates the Nintendo logo data in the cartridge header at boot, similar to the Game Boy logo check.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_VirtualBoyRegionDesc
data.value: Virtual Boy hardware is region-free; however, all commercial titles were released only in Japan and North America.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_WataraRegionDesc
data.value: Watara Supervision is region-free; the handheld was sold worldwide with no regional restrictions.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_WiiDiscEncryptDesc
data.value: Wii discs use AES-128-CBC encryption with per-title keys stored in an encrypted title key block. The common key is stored in the console's OTP memory. Additionally, disc authenticity is verified via a signature chain (CA → CP → Ticket).

data.name: SecAnalyzer_WiiRegionLockDesc
data.value: Wii enforces region lockout at both hardware and software levels. The System Menu validates the disc region before launching.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_GameCubeRegionLockDynDesc
data.value: Region: {0}. GameCube enforces region lockout via the Game ID in the disc header. The console checks the region code and refuses to boot discs from a different region.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_GbaHeaderCheckDynDesc
data.value: Header checksum: {0}. The GBA BIOS validates the header checksum before booting.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_MegaDriveCheckDynDesc
data.value: Internal checksum: {0}. Some Mega Drive games validate this checksum at boot.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_MegaDriveRegionDynDesc
data.value: Region code: "{0}". {1}. Mega Drive uses hardware region lockout.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_N64CicBootDynDesc
data.value: Boot code hash: 0x{0}. N64 CIC chip validates boot code for anti-piracy and region locking.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_N64CrcDynDesc
data.value: Header CRC1: {0}, CRC2: {1}. N64 boot code validates these checksums.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_N64RegionCodeDynDesc
data.value: Region: {0} (0x{1}). N64 uses CIC chip for region lockout.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_NdsHeaderCrcDynDesc
data.value: Header CRC16: {0}. The DS firmware validates this CRC16 checksum (bytes 0x00-0x15D) before booting.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SMSGameGearRegionDynDesc
data.value: Header region: {0}. Region is encoded in the TMR SEGA header and enforced by BIOS on consoles that have one.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_Sega32xCheckDynDesc
data.value: Internal checksum: {0}. The 32X uses a Mega Drive-compatible checksum in the header.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SegaCDRegionDynDesc
data.value: Region code: "{0}". {1}. Sega CD uses region coding in the disc header similar to Mega Drive.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SmsGgChecksumDynDesc
data.value: TMR SEGA checksum: {0}. Consoles with a BIOS validate this checksum before running the game.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_SnesRegionLockDynDesc
data.value: Region code: 0x{0} ({1}). SNES CIC chip enforces region-based lockout.

data.name: SecAnalyzer_WiiRegionLockDynDesc
data.value: Region: {0}. Wii enforces region lockout at both hardware and software levels. The System Menu checks the disc region code against the console's region setting.

data.name: GpMapper_Description
data.value: Map gamepad buttons to keyboard keys, mouse actions, scripts, and macros. Create multiple switchable mapping sets and auto-profiles that activate based on the active application.

data.name: GpMapper_MappingsHint
data.value: Click Assign to map a gamepad input to a keyboard key, mouse button, mouse movement, script, or macro.

data.name: GpMapper_MacroHint
data.value: One step per line. Format: Key:A 50ms, Mouse:Left 100ms, Run:path args. The delay after each step defaults to 50ms.

data.name: GpMapper_AutoProfilesHint
data.value: Automatically switch to a profile when a matching application window gains focus.

data.name: GpMapper_WizardDesc
data.value: Quickly set up a new profile with common mappings using the step-by-step wizard.

data.name: GpMapper_WizardStep1Desc
data.value: Choose a name for your new mapping profile.

data.name: GpMapper_WizardStep2Desc
data.value: Assign keyboard keys to the face buttons (A, B, X, Y).

data.name: GpMapper_WizardStep3Desc
data.value: Choose what the D-Pad should do.

data.name: GpMapper_WizardStep4Desc
data.value: Choose what the left analog stick should do.

data.name: GpMapper_WizardStep5Desc
data.value: Assign keyboard keys to the triggers and shoulder buttons.

data.name: MameAuditor_SummaryFormat
data.value: {0} good, {1} incomplete, {2} bad out of {3} ROM sets. {4} machines missing from directory.

data.name: MameChdConv_ChdmanNotFound
data.value: chdman not found. Please install MAME tools (chdman) and ensure it is available in your system PATH, or configure your MAME path in Settings.

data.name: MameChd_HashNotAvailable
data.value: Hash verification not available (no raw SHA-1 in header)

data.name: MameChd_SummaryFormat
data.value: {0} valid, {1} invalid out of {2} CHD files.

data.name: MameDatEditor_SummaryFormat
data.value: {0} games ({1} parents, {2} clones), {3} ROMs, {4} disks, {5} samples

data.name: MameLauncher_MameNotFound
data.value: MAME not found. Please configure the path in Settings or download MAME.

data.name: MameRebuilder_ProgressIndexed
data.value: Indexed {0} files, found {1} unique CRC32 values.

data.name: MameRebuilder_SummaryFormat
data.value: {0} complete, {1} partial, {2} failed, {3} skipped out of {4} machines.

data.name: MameSamples_SummaryFormat
data.value: {0} good, {1} incomplete, {2} bad out of {3} sample sets. {4} sets missing from directory.

data.name: Browser_ExportDestinationTitle
data.value: Select Output Folder for Header Export

data.name: Browser_ConfirmDeletePrompt
data.value: ⚠️ Are you sure you want to permanently delete {0} ROM file(s)? This cannot be undone.

data.name: FormatConv_ConvertToCHD
data.value: Convert to CHD (Compressed Hunks of Data)

data.name: FormatConv_ErrorTooSmall
data.value: File is too small to contain a {0}.

data.name: FormatConv_ErrorNotNes
data.value: Not a valid iNES ROM file.

data.name: FormatConv_ErrorTooLargeNes
data.value: File is too large ({0:F1} MB). Maximum supported size for NES header fix: 64 MB.

data.name: FormatConv_ErrorChdmanNotFound
data.value: chdman not found. Please install MAME tools (chdman) and ensure it is available in your system PATH.

data.name: FormatConv_ErrorDolphinToolNotFound
data.value: DolphinTool not found. Please install Dolphin Emulator tools (DolphinTool) and ensure it is available in your system PATH.

data.name: SaveConv_ErrorFileTooLarge
data.value: File is too large ({0:F1} MB). Maximum supported size: {1} MB.

data.name: N64Conv_ProgressAlreadyTarget
data.value: Source ROM is already in the target format. Copying...

data.name: N64Conv_ErrorUnknownFormat
data.value: Not a recognized N64 ROM (unknown byte order).

data.name: AnaloguePocket_SelectRestoreFolder
data.value: Select Backup Folder to Restore From

data.name: AnaloguePocket_SelectGbCameraOutput
data.value: Select Output Folder for GB Camera Photos

data.name: AnaloguePocket_SelectCopySource
data.value: Select Source Folder to Copy From

data.name: Analogue3D_ConfirmDeleteMessage
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete "{0}" and all its associated settings and artwork? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: AnalogueMegaSg_BrowseSdCardTitle
data.value: Select Analogue Mega SG SD Card

data.name: AnalogueNtSuperNt_BrowseSdCardTitle
data.value: Select Analogue NT / Super NT SD Card

data.name: RetroArchLauncher_RetroArchNotFound
data.value: RetroArch not found. Please configure the path in Settings or download RetroArch.

data.name: RetroArchLauncher_NoCoreMapping
data.value: No RetroArch core mapping found for system: {0}

data.name: RetroArchLauncher_CoreNotFound
data.value: Core '{0}' not found. Please install the '{0}' core in RetroArch (Online Updater → Core Downloader).

data.name: MednafenLauncher_MednafenNotFound
data.value: Mednafen not found. Please configure the path in Settings or download Mednafen.

data.name: MednafenLauncher_NoModuleMapping
data.value: No Mednafen module mapping found for system: {0}
